exclude_note: The two exclude-list rules (-a always,exclude -F msgtype=PROCTITLE and -F msgtype=PATH) SUPPRESS their named record types from every audit event group (f3 section 2.3/3.5). PROCTITLE is appended to execve-group events (~100 B); PATH is emitted for file-path operations (~200 B). Both are direction=suppressive and contribute 0 additive volume. The additive rules (execve, two watches) produce the events that these excludes then trim.

rules.note: Drops PROCTITLE sub-records (~100 B each, AUDIT_PROCTITLE=1327) from execve-group events. Contributes 0 additive volume. Reduces per-execve-event bytes by ~100 B (1200 -> ~1100 B). A naive impl that treats 'always,exclude' as 'always,exit' counts this as additive MEDIUM volume - that is the trap.

Drops PATH sub-records (~200 B each, AUDIT_PATH=1302) from file-touch event groups. Contributes 0 additive volume. Reduces per-watch-event bytes by ~200 B (900 -> ~700 B). Same trap as PROCTITLE exclude above: a naive impl adds a spurious MEDIUM band.

auid-filtered execve: ~0.3x the unrestricted form (MEDIUM per f3 section 6). 1200 B/event base; PROCTITLE exclude reduces effective bytes to ~1100 B/event. The rate band is the same as unrestricted * 0.3 per f3 section 6.
Single-file watch on a hardened host (few writes/day). 900 B/event base (no EXECVE record, f3 section 3.3); PATH exclude reduces effective bytes to ~700 B/event.

Same as /etc/passwd watch above. Same key 'identity' (shared key; per-key aggregation must attribute both watches together, not conflate as a single rule). 900 B/event base; PATH exclude reduces effective bytes to ~700 B/event.

dominant_tier: MEDIUM (additive execve rule), with two NEGATIVE/suppressive excludes reducing per-event bytes
rate_band_basis: f3 section 6: auid-filtered execve MEDIUM (low1.5k/typ15k/high150k). Watches LOW (single hardened-host files, few writes/day). Excludes NEGATIVE/suppressive (0 additive, reduce bytes).
